title: Duck boat overturns in Cambridge, injuring 11 people
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A Boston duck boat overturned in Cambridge on Saturday injuring 11 people, one seriously, at a ramp near the Charles River.
The vehicle flipped while being towed after a mechanical issue; the tow rope snapped causing it to tip over.
Boston Duck Tours suspended operations for the rest of the day and is cooperating with State Police and Coast Guard investigations.

title: Why JJ Peterka was worth the price of 2 1st-round picks for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins traded two first-round picks for 24-year-old winger JJ Peterka from Utah Mammoth to boost scoring.
Peterka averaged 26 goals over the last three seasons and brings top-six potential to the Bruins lineup.
Despite a recent scoring dip, Peterka ranked high in 5-on-5 productivity and is known for his speed and offensive skills.

title: MassHealth will stop covering weight-loss drugs next week
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: MassHealth will stop covering GLP-1 and anti-obesity drugs for weight loss starting July 1, affecting 22,000 members.
The policy change aims to save the state $15 million annually as part of Governor Maura Healey's fiscal 2027 budget.
Wegovy and Zepbound prices range from $1,000 to over $1,300 monthly, with some programs offering discounts for self-pay.

title: Here’s the 7 prospects Bruins added on Day 2 of 2026 NHL Draft
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins selected seven players in Rounds 2-7 of the 2026 NHL Draft.
Boston acquired winger JJ Peterka by trading its 2026 and a 2028 first-round pick.
Notable picks include goalie Yuri Ivanov (Round 2) and forward Nils Bartholdsson (Round 3).

title: Native Venezuelan Willson Contreras speaks on devastating earthquakes: ‘I always keep Venezuela in my heart’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Two earthquakes in Venezuela resulted in 1,430 deaths and 68,900 missing as of Saturday.
Red Sox players, including Venezuelan natives, wore personalized messages supporting Venezuelan earthquake victims.
David Ortiz raised awareness and funds for Venezuelan relief efforts at Fenway Park before a Red Sox game.

title: Jaylen Brown responds to report that he’d be the 7th-best player on an NBA team according to analytics
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown disputed an analytics report ranking him as the 7th-best player on an NBA team via social media.
NBA analyst Bobby Marks cited mixed team views on Brown due to analytics, sparking online debate.
Brown, rumored in trade talks, remains highly valued, with many teams interested despite the analytics report.

title: Red Sox jump on Gerrit Cole early to defeat Yankees in third straight game
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox defeat Yankees 4-1, securing their third consecutive win against A.L.-leading New York.
Jake Bennett held Yankees hitless into the fifth inning, allowing one run over 6.1 innings for his second win.
Gerrit Cole's career ERA against Boston rises above 6 after conceding four runs in 5.1 innings.

title: Bruins trade Fabian Lysell to Colorado for forward Ivan Ivan
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins traded Fabian Lysell to the Colorado Avalanche for forward Ivan Ivan.
Fabian Lysell played 12 NHL games, scoring one goal and two assists for the Bruins.
Ivan Ivan, 23, recorded 26 points in 66 games with the Colorado Eagles during 2025-26 season.

title: Forget the celebrities in the crowd, France are the entertainers of the 2026 World Cup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France defeated Norway 4-1 in a World Cup match at Gillette Stadium in Foxborough.
Kylian Mbappe and Ousmane Dembélé led France's dynamic attack, contributing assists and goals.
Despite a higher xG, Norway couldn't overcome France's superior quality in the attacking third.

title: Cape DA investigating alleged threats at Sandwich Middle High
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A school safety app flagged a threat at Sandwich Middle High, prompting an investigation by local police and district attorney.
Authorities are investigating an alleged threat made by a student against two classmates; details remain undisclosed due to juvenile involvement.
The student involved has been removed from school, and families of targeted students have been notified, ensuring community safety.
